title: Plugin System Core
description: Architect a generic plugin loader that allows third-party modules to extend application functionality while maintaining strict type safety.
requirements: Designing highly decoupled and extensible software architectures.
Using Advanced Types (Conditional, Mapped) for flexible APIs.
Managing complex object lifecycles with strict type safety.
Applying OOP principles (Classes, Inheritance) at scale.
hints: Plugin contexts are often best defined as a union of multiple service interfaces.
Conditional types can be used to ensure that a plugin's required context matches the host's provided context.
Think about how VS Code or Figma allow developers to build extensions—that's the pattern you're aiming for!
expectedOutput: A rock-solid foundational core that enables other developers to build features for your application with full IDE support and zero type errors.
